title: Swiss Travel Pass vs Half Fare Card
summary: A practical comparison for first-time Switzerland travelers who want to choose the right rail discount option without overpaying.
body:
If you are planning train travel in Switzerland, the Swiss Travel Pass and the Half Fare Card solve different problems.

The Swiss Travel Pass is usually better for travelers who want simple unlimited travel on consecutive days and do not want to calculate each ticket. The Half Fare Card is usually better for travelers who want lower upfront cost and are happy to buy discounted point-to-point tickets.

The best choice depends on your route, how many long-distance train days you have, and whether you also plan to use mountain transport frequently.

For most first-time visitors, the practical approach is to compare total expected ticket costs before buying either option.
